data_in.description: A filtered mpactr R6 object that has undergone filter_mispicked_ions with merge_peaks=TRUE and merge_method='sum'

data_out.description: Data table of ions that failed the mispicked filter (removed or merged due to isotopic pattern misidentification)
Data table of ions that passed the mispicked filter (retained in feature table)

domain_knowledge: Mispicked ions result from incorrect splitting of isotopic patterns during peak picking or detector saturation artifacts in tandem MS/MS preprocessing.
filter_summary() returns a named list with separate vectors for failed and passed ion identifiers, enabling downstream tracking of ion fate through the filtering pipeline.
Merge method 'sum' combines intensity values from similar ions during mispicked ion consolidation, which affects the final feature table abundance values.
Reference semantics in mpactr R6 objects means filter_summary() inspects the in-place modified state; separate copies are not needed to track filter history.

direct_checks: verify file exists in mums2/mpactr repository: vignette or documentation describing filter_summary() applied to 'mispicked' filter output structure

script_runs: R code loading mpactr library, importing cultures_peak_table.csv and cultures_metadata.csv via example_path(), applying filter_mispicked_ions(merge_peaks = TRUE, merge_method = "sum"), then calling filter_summary() on the result
output_matches_reference: verify returned object contains exactly two named lists with keys 'passed_ions' and 'failed_ions' (or equivalent), robust to column ordering within each list
file_format_is: both passed_ions and failed_ions outputs are data tables (data.frame or data.table class)
row_count_equals: verify row counts in passed_ions and failed_ions tables are non-negative integers and sum to total number of ions in input peak table
expert_review: verify structure and field names of passed_ions and failed_ions tables match the vignette specification for the 'mispicked' filter (requires domain knowledge of expected ion metadata fields)
assess whether filter_summary() output correctly identifies ions that failed vs. passed the mispicked-ion detection logic as documented in the vignette

evidence_snippets.text: [results] What is the structure and content of the output returned by filter_summary() when applied to a filtered mpactr object with the 'mispicked' filter?: 'If you are interested in viewing the passing and failing ions for a single filter, use the `filter_summary()` function.  You must specify which filter you are interested in, either "mispicked",'

[results] filter_summary() returns a named list containing two components: failed_ions and passed_ions, both of which can be displayed as data tables using head() to view a subset of ions.: 'Failed ions: 
```{r}
head(mispicked_summary$failed_ions, 100)
```

Passing ions:
```{r}
head(mispicked_summary$passed_ions, 100)
```'

[methods] A filtered mpactr R6 object that has undergone filter_mispicked_ions with merge_peaks=TRUE and merge_method='sum': 'data_filtered <- data |> filter_mispicked_ions(merge_peaks = TRUE, merge_method = "sum")'
[methods] Data table of ions that failed the mispicked filter (removed or merged due to isotopic pattern misidentification): 'filter_summary(data_mispicked, "mispicked")$failed_ions'
[methods] Data table of ions that passed the mispicked filter (retained in feature table): 'filter_summary(data_mispicked, "mispicked")$passed_ions'
[methods] R: 'This table can be used for a variety of analyses that can be conducted in R'
[discussion] No changelog documenting mpactr versions, filter_summary() function signature changes, or expected output schema over time.: 'No changelog found.'

cmd: library(mpactr); library(data.table); obj <- readRDS('{inputs.filtered_mpactr_obj}'); summary_result <- obj$filter_summary(filter='mispicked'); failed_ions_dt <- data.table(ion_id=summary_result$failed_ions, filter_status='failed'); passed_ions_dt <- data.table(ion_id=summary_result$passed_ions, filter_status='passed'); saveRDS(failed_ions_dt, 'failed_ions_mispicked.rds'); saveRDS(passed_ions_dt, 'passed_ions_mispicked.rds'); cat('Filter summary extraction complete\n')
executable.grounding_notes: Unverified by the executable guard — command runs inline code (-c/-e); its API calls cannot be validated against package files. Treat this command as a best-effort inference; verify the entry point and APIs against the real code before running.

expected_outputs: Data table of ions that failed the mispicked filter (removed or merged due to isotopic pattern misidentification)
Data table of ions that passed the mispicked filter (retained in feature table)
finding: filter_summary() returns a named list containing two components: failed_ions and passed_ions, both of which can be displayed as data tables using head() to view a subset of ions.
inputs: A filtered mpactr R6 object that has undergone filter_mispicked_ions with merge_peaks=TRUE and merge_method='sum'
methodology_summary: Load the filtered mpactr object that has undergone mispicked ions filter with merge_peaks=TRUE and merge_method='sum'.
Invoke filter_summary() method on the mpactr object with filter parameter set to 'mispicked' to retrieve the named list of ion fate outcomes.
Extract failed_ions and passed_ions vectors from the returned named list.
Convert each vector to a data.table structure with compound identifiers and corresponding filter status columns.
Validation: Confirm that the sum of failed and passed ion counts equals the total number of input ions processed by the filter, and that all compound identifiers are non-empty strings.
missing_information: No changelog documenting mpactr versions, filter_summary() function signature changes, or expected output schema over time.
research_question: What is the structure and content of the output returned by filter_summary() when applied to a filtered mpactr object with the 'mispicked' filter?

task_description: Apply filter_summary() to a filtered mpactr object to retrieve the ion fate data (passed and failed ions) for the 'mispicked' filter and output two structured data tables.

task_inputs.label: Filtered mpactr object after mispicked ions filtering

task_objective: Execute filter_summary() on a filtered mpactr object with the 'mispicked' filter and verify the returned named list contains properly structured failed_ions and passed_ions data tables.

task_outputs.label: Data table of ions flagged and removed by mispicked filter
Data table of ions passing the mispicked filter

title: Reproduce ion filtering summary via filter_summary() for mispicked ions filter

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: Data table of ions that failed the mispicked filter (removed or merged due to isotopic pattern misidentification), Data table of ions that passed the mispicked filter (retained in feature table).
workflow_description: 1. Load a filtered mpactr object (previously processed with filter_mispicked_ions). 2. Call filter_summary(data_object, 'mispicked') to extract a named list containing failed_ions and passed_ions vectors. 3. Convert each vector to a data.table with compound identifiers and filter status columns. 4. Export both tables to CSV format for downstream analysis or visualization.
